title: The "Process of Process": EBA in the Process Manufacturing Industries
author: Aberdeen Group
source_file: 1998 The "Process of Process"- EBA in the Process Manufacturing Industries.pdf
abstract: This 90-page Aberdeen Group report examines Enterprise Business Applications (EBA) for the process manufacturing sector, including food, chemical, pharmaceutical, and other continuous-process industries. It evaluates major EBA suppliers, their core products, key alliances, and vertical industry targeting, while identifying the unique IT requirements of process manufacturers including potency control, temperature regulation, shelf life, catch-weight management, and regulatory compliance tracking. The report benchmarks market dynamics and critical success factors for both users and suppliers of process-specific enterprise software.
importance_rationale: Aberdeen was among the first analyst firms to systematically distinguish process-specific EBA needs from discrete manufacturing ERP, addressing a major gap in the late-1990s market research landscape where most ERP analysis treated manufacturing as monolithic.
relevance_rationale: The analytical framework distinguishing process from discrete manufacturing needs remains conceptually valid; specific vendor profiles and market-share data are dated but the category definitions and success factors are still referenced in modern food/chemical ERP vendor literature.
prescience_rationale: The report correctly identified that many leading ERP vendors lacked genuine process-manufacturing depth; SAP and Oracle ultimately closed these gaps through acquisition and module extension, validating the prediction that specialist vendors would face platform competition.
